data_flow_trace.original: Attacker compromises the staker private key (phishing, key leak, malware).
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: renewLockUntil is gated by onlyStaker modifier; same access model as before.
The very first check rejects any durationMicros > MAX_LOCKUP_DURATION (4 * 365 days * 1e6 microseconds = 4 years).
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: CHANGED: previous code had no cap (only the TODO(yxia) comment). Current code adds a hard 4-year cap via MAX_LOCKUP_DURATION constant defined at StakePool.sol:35.

data_flow_trace.original: lockedUntil is set to type(uint64).max - 1 (~584,942 years from Unix epoch).
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: Unreachable: any duration large enough to push lockedUntil beyond now + 4 years is rejected at step 2. Each call can at most add 4 years to the existing lockedUntil, and the overflow check at L467 still prevents wraparound.
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: MAX_LOCKUP_DURATION cap at L461, overflow check at L467, min-lockup floor at L474.
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: Because durationMicros is capped at ~4 years, lockedUntil can never jump to type(uint64).max-1 in one call. Even repeated calls only move lockedUntil forward in 4-year increments from the current lockedUntil, not from now, so attacker can still slowly accumulate lockup; see counterargument.

data_flow_trace.original: All overflow and minimum-duration checks pass.
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: Overflow and min-lockup checks are still there, plus the new max-lockup cap executes before them.
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: Additional max-cap guard added before the existing checks.

data_flow_trace.original: All funds in activeStake are permanently locked. Future addStake() deposits are also trapped.
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: addStake still extends lockup to max(current, now + minLockup), but with the per-call 4-year cap the worst attainable permanent lock is only 4 years from the last successful renew (requires repeated staker calls with time passing to grow beyond that).
data_flow_trace.gating_conditions: Same onlyStaker gate; no new floor-reset path, but no catastrophic centuries-long lock either.
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: Permanent fund destruction claim no longer holds; worst case is bounded by MAX_LOCKUP_DURATION per call.

data_flow_trace.original: _unstake() creates pending buckets inheriting this lockedUntil, so even unstaked funds require now > lockedUntil + unbondingDelay.
data_flow_trace.current_state_summary: Pending bucket logic is unchanged; however the input lockedUntil it inherits is now bounded by the 4-year cap rather than uint64.max.
data_flow_trace.differs_from_original: Indirectly mitigated by the upstream cap.

reasoning_chain: The vulnerability has been fixed by the explicit TODO(yxia) the issue itself flagged. At StakePool.sol:461-463 the function now rejects any `durationMicros > MAX_LOCKUP_DURATION`, where MAX_LOCKUP_DURATION is defined at StakePool.sol:35 as `uint64(4 * 365 days) * 1_000_000` (~4 years in microseconds). The exploit requires passing `type(uint64).max - lockedUntil - 1`, which is orders of magnitude larger than 4 years, so the very first guard in the function reverts with `Errors.ExcessiveLockupDuration`. The attack chain is definitively broken at step 2.

key_evidence.excerpt:
/// @notice Maximum allowed lockup duration (4 years in microseconds)
    uint64 public constant MAX_LOCKUP_DURATION = uint64(4 * 365 days) * 1_000_000;
key_evidence.why_matters: Defines the hard cap the issue's TODO requested.
The TODO(yxia) has been replaced with an actual max-lockup protection check as the very first statement in the function.
Corresponding error type exists and is used by the new guard.

counterargument: A determined compromised staker could still call renewLockUntil repeatedly (each call adds up to 4 years to lockedUntil, compounding monotonically). Over time, and if the staker waits for `lockedUntil` to approach `now`, each successive renew could extend the lock by 4 more years, so the theoretical reachable lockedUntil still grows unbounded across many transactions. However: (a) each successful call requires the staker remaining compromised and wall-clock time passing, (b) the governance owner can rotate the staker via setStaker() between calls to stop further extension, and (c) the 4-year per-call cap converts the issue from 'one transaction permanently destroys funds for ~584k years' into 'funds have at most 4 years added per transaction', which is qualitatively different and not the critical single-shot fund-destruction the issue describes. The issue text explicitly frames criticality as 'A single unauthorized transaction is sufficient' — that property is no longer true.
